channel.title: Supabase on Joe Muller's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Supabase on Joe Muller's Blog

item.title: Supabase Slack Notifications
item.description:
Slack

1. Create a new Slack channel

Create a slack channel in your workspace where the notifications will be displayed (ex. “notifications”)

2. Create a Slack app

Visit the Slack API webpage and “Create New App”. Give the app a name and select the Slack workspace you’re working with.

3. Configure the Slack app

Select “Incoming Webhooks” and flip the “Active Incoming Webhooks” switch on.

4. Create the webhook

Add New Webhook to Workspace and select the “notifications” channel you created before.

item.title: Using Supabase in Typescript
item.description:
Instead of hardcoding Supabase interfaces or types in your TypeScript projects, you can use the Supabase CLI to generate types directly from your database tables. Make sure to add the output inside your src folder so the rest of your application can access it:

supabase gen types typescript --project-id abcdefghijklmnopqrst > src/types/database.types.ts

This command will generate a .types.ts file that contains a single Database interface containing all of your tables and their columns. The output looks something like this (I pulled this from the official docs):

item.title: Crashify AI
item.description:
• Website: https://crashify.ai
  • Status: On hold
  • Stack:
    • TypeScript
    • React
    • Supabase

Crashify AI is a website that lets you generate 10-part “../../posts/projects/crashify"crash courses.

item.title: AI Co-Founded
item.description:
• Website: https://aicofounded.com
  • Status: On hold
  • Stack:
    • TypeScript
    • React
    • Supabase

AI Co-Founded is a website that helps you generate business plans.
